criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified

The cherry-picking scheme is identified as a distinct allegation throughout the document.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not cite Section 206(1) or Section 206(2) of the Investment Advisers Act in connection with the cherry-picking allegation. Section 206(2) is only mentioned once in the context of the 'Deficient Written Policies and Procedures' section, not in connection with the cherry-picking allegation itself. The core cherry-picking fraud provisions — Sections 206(1) and 206(2) — are never cited as the statutory basis for the cherry-picking scheme.

The output focuses entirely on WCA, Whitmore, Chao, cherry-picking, recordkeeping failures, and obstruction allegations. Bellini's personal exposure is completely absent from the work product.

criteria_results.title: Total Alleged Ill-Gotten Gains / Client Harm Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output only mentions the $8,238,000 cherry-picking figure (in the 'Direct Financial Harm to Clients' section) but does not provide an aggregate total of approximately $8.622 million that combines cherry-picking gains ($8,238,000) + excess commissions ($291,400) + undisclosed compensation ($92,600). No aggregate monetary figure across all allegation categories is calculated or stated anywhere in the output.

criteria_results.title: Hargrove & Tilton Review: Failure to Test Trade Allocation Noted
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output mentions the Hargrove & Tilton annual compliance review dated December 8, 2023 (Exhibit G) in the context of WCA lacking adequate records for trade allocation decisions, but it does not note that the review failed to test trade allocation patterns despite the firm's heavy reliance on omnibus trading. The criterion specifically requires identifying this omission as relevant evidence of WCA's deficient compliance infrastructure, which the agent's output does not address.

criteria_results.title: Key Dates: Examination Timeline Accurately Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output only explicitly states two of the five required dates: the examination commenced March 11, 2024 (mentioned in Categories 6 and the backdated spreadsheets section), and the deletion instruction occurred on April 22, 2024 (not one of the five required dates). The referral notice date of November 6, 2024 appears in a citation reference ('100 F Street, N.E. Washington, D.C. November 6, 2024') but is not clearly stated as the referral notice date. The deficiency letter date (August 2, 2024), the referral to Enforcement date (October 18, 2024), and the service date (November 12, 2024) are not mentioned anywhere in the output. The criterion requires at least three of the five dates to be correctly stated, and the output fails to meet this threshold.

criteria_results.title: Whitmore's 12% LP Interest Value Correctly Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output mentions Whitmore's limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP but states it is valued at approximately '$' (the dollar amount is blank/missing) as of December 31, 2023. The specific value of approximately $22.2 million (12% × $185M) is never stated in the output. The percentage (12%) and the calculated value ($22.2 million) are both absent from the document.

criteria_results.title: Compliance Manual Last Updated November 15, 2022
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not mention the Compliance Manual being last updated on November 15, 2022. While the Compliance Manual is referenced multiple times throughout the output (citing various sections like 7.3 and 9.1), no specific last-updated date of November 15, 2022 is mentioned anywhere in the document.

criteria_results.title: Lakeshore Trading LLC: Ostrowski's 40% Ownership Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output contains no mention of Lakeshore Trading LLC or Kevin Ostrowski anywhere in the document. The output focuses on WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors), its principals Whitmore and Chao, and various SEC allegations related to cherry-picking, recordkeeping failures, and obstructive conduct. There is no reference to Lakeshore Trading LLC or a 40% ownership stake by Kevin Ostrowski.

criteria_results.title: Lakeshore Trading LLC: Ostrowski-Bellini Personal Relationship and Undisclosed Nature
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output contains no mention of Lakeshore Trading LLC, Ostrowski, Bellini, or any college roommate/personal relationship. The output focuses entirely on Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC (WCA) and its alleged violations. The criterion requires identification of the Ostrowski-Bellini personal relationship and its undisclosed nature, which is completely absent from the output.

criteria_results.title: Email Exhibit Language Quoted or Paraphrased
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not reference the Whitmore-to-Delgado email (whitmore-delgado-email.eml) at all, nor does it mention any ambiguous language such as 'clean up the shared drive' or 'clean up of legacy files on the shared drive' that could support an innocent interpretation. While the output discusses Whitmore instructing Delgado to delete folders, it does not cite or quote the specific email exhibit or note its potentially ambiguous language.
